articles.title: The Download: Europe’s heat wave hits the grid, and IBM’s chip targets Moore’s Law
articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Europe’s extreme heat is shutting down power plants Europe is in the middle of a record-breaking heat wave, and the grid is being pushed to its limits as people turn to…

articles.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
articles.summary: A coordinated law enforcement operation, in partnership with private sector companies, including Bitdefender, Bitsight, ESET, and Microsoft, has resulted in the takedown of criminal infrastructure powering Amadey and StealC. "The main common goal was to disrupt the 'assembly lines' cybercriminals use to launch ransomware, financial fraud, and attacks on critical infrastructure," Europol said in

articles.title: IBM has unveiled chip technology that could help extend Moore’s Law another decade
articles.summary: IBM has built a new prototype chip with around 100 billion transistors on an area the size of a fingernail, which is twice the density of the company’s previous state-of-the-art technology announced in 2021. The design could pave the way for faster and more energy efficient computers for years to come. For more than half…

articles.title: New Mistic Backdoor Linked to KongTuke in ClickFix and ModeloRAT Campaigns
articles.summary: A new, stealthy backdoor named Mistic has been deployed as part of suspected financially motivated attacks aimed at multiple organizations spanning insurance, education, IT, and professional services sectors since April 2026. According to Symantec and Carbon Black's Threat Hunter Team, the backdoor, also tracked as MLTBackdoor, is said to be linked to an initial access broker (IAB) named

articles.title: CISA Warns Critical Lantronix EDS5000 Flaw Is Being Actively Exploited
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday warned of active exploitation of a critical security flaw impacting Lantronix EDS5000 Series devices, urging Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by June 26, 2026. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2025-67038 (CVSS score: 9.8), a code injection flaw that could result in the execution

articles.title: Europe's heatwave shifts east as France raises health alert to highest level
articles.summary: Temperatures in Germany could hit 40C in some areas while French officials say deaths linked to the heat are being seen among young people.

articles.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
articles.summary: Signs that traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz shipping route is gradually resuming has helped to push the oil price down.

articles.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities
articles.summary: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.

articles.title: Heat pump growth stalls as government support cut, warns climate watchdog
articles.summary: The growth in sales has slowed significantly after a critical government grant programme was cut.

articles.title: Central African Republic peace gains must be preserved: UN envoy
articles.summary: The Central African Republic (CAR) “has made remarkable and tangible progress towards lasting peace and security” in recent years, but security remains fragile in border areas, including with war-torn Sudan, UN Special Representative Valentine Rugwabiza told the Security Council on Tuesday.

articles.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Zero-Day CVE-2026-20245 Exploited to Gain Root Access
articles.summary: An unknown threat actor exploited a recently disclosed high-severity security flaw impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN as a zero-day at least two months before it was publicly disclosed, according to new findings from Google-owned Mandiant. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS score: 7.8), allows an authenticated, local attacker to execute arbitrary commands with elevated privileges

articles.title: Can the circular economy win over big business?
articles.summary: Policymakers and businesses are turning to the circular economy in a bid to increase self-reliance and reduce environmental impacts The post Can the circular economy win over big business? appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: EU, UK lead push for electrification as “powerful weapon” against fossil fuels
articles.summary: A group of dozens of countries seeks to build momentum for a COP31 pledge to electrify 35% of global energy use by 2035 as an alternative to fossil fuels The post EU, UK lead push for electrification as “powerful weapon” against fossil fuels appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: UN asks AI companies to reveal full environmental impacts
articles.summary: UN chief António Guterres says big AI firms should disclose the emissions, water and energy use of their data centres amid community opposition The post UN asks AI companies to reveal full environmental impacts appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
articles.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

articles.title: Key figure in South Africa police corruption scandal pleads guilty
articles.summary: Prosecutors say Vusimusi "Cat" Matlala could provide evidence against "high-ranking officials".

articles.title: Police seal off key roads in Nairobi as Kenyans march to remember protest victims
articles.summary: Protesters demand justice for more than 80 people killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

articles.title: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on children and armed conflict
articles.summary: The UN Security Council held on open debate on Thursday focused on strengthening protections for children caught in armed conflict. The Secretary-General's latest report reveals that in 2025, soldiers and Government forces were responsible for more grave violations against children in armed conflict than non-State armed groups, marking a first in 30 years of UN monitoring. The report verified 38,558 grave violations such as killing, recruitment and abduction, affecting 24,174 children, many of w

articles.title: Report details widespread use of sexual violence in Sudan war
articles.summary: A new UN human rights report issued on Tuesday details the brutality and scale of conflict-related sexual violence in Sudan since war erupted in April 2023 and its profound, long-term impacts on victims, families and communities.

articles.title: NASA at the Ion: Orion Lessons from Artemis II Shape NASA’s Moon to Mars Path
articles.summary: Seven weeks after the Orion spacecraft returned four astronauts from humanity’s first crewed journey around the Moon since Apollo, Artemis II Orion Vehicle Manager Branelle Rodriguez reflected on the mission’s achievements and how it is shaping NASA’s return to the lunar surface and future missions to Mars. Introduced by NASA’s Johnson Space Center Acting Director of Business Development and Technology Integration Monte Goforth, Rodriguez spoke at the Ion in […]

articles.title: Euclid View of Milky Way Heart Previews Core Survey by NASA’s Roman
articles.summary: A new look at the heart of our Milky Way galaxy by Euclid, an ESA (European Space Agency) mission with NASA contributions, overlaps with a region scientists will observe with NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, launching later this summer. This sneak peek gives astronomers a major jumpstart on a core Roman survey, helping scientists […]

articles.title: Europe’s extreme heat is shutting down power plants
articles.summary: Europe is in the middle of a record-breaking heat wave, and the grid is being pushed to its limits as people turn to fans and air-conditioning to try to stay cool. Some power plants won’t be online to help handle the load. On June 23, France saw its hottest day since record-keeping began in 1947.…

articles.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new class of CI/CD workflow weakness that allows attackers to hijack workflows and compromise open-source supply chains. The "critical exploitable pattern" has been codenamed Cordyceps by Novee Security. The issue can allow full attacker control of repositories at dozens of the largest organizations worldwide, including Microsoft, Google, Apache, and

articles.title: Did Colombia’s energy transition just come to a halt?
articles.summary: With promises to lift a ban on oil and gas exploration and scale back renewables support, Colombia’s new Tiger President could threaten the country’s clean energy shift The post Did Colombia’s energy transition just come to a halt? appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: UN chief says fossil fuel industry must cut methane for warming “relief”
articles.summary: The UN Secretary-General urges drastic reductions in methane emissions, starting with the energy sector, to limit an expected overshoot of global warming beyond 1.5C The post UN chief says fossil fuel industry must cut methane for warming “relief” appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: China’s coal-chemicals boom risks repeating the mistakes of the past
articles.summary: By reviving the problematic industry in the name of energy security, China runs the risk of higher emissions, stranded assets and setting back its ambitions as a clean energy leader The post China’s coal-chemicals boom risks repeating the mistakes of the past appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.summary: Despite recent shifts in federal energy policies, our analysis shows that the US transition to... The post Guest post: How US renewable-energy growth persists despite federal policy uncertainty appeared first on Carbon Brief .

articles.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
articles.summary: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

articles.title: IBM hails new 'block of flats' design breakthrough for ultra tiny chips
articles.summary: IBM says it has created the world's first known chip tech below 1 nanometre - but it will be some time before it's ready for production.

articles.title: In pictures: Britain braves record breaking June temperatures
articles.summary: A rare red weather warning is in place as extreme temperatures continue across much of the UK.

articles.title: Hottest June day on record for the UK as temperature reaches 36C
articles.summary: A rare red warning for extreme heat is in effect as soaring temperatures continue

articles.title: World News in Brief: Students injured in Sudan drone strike, dangerous returns in south Lebanon, celebrating women diplomats
articles.summary: Ongoing fighting in the Sudanese city of El Obeid continues to endanger civilians and damage infrastructure, the United Nations said on Wednesday.

articles.title: UN chief: Targeting methane is a climate battle ‘we can win’
articles.summary: Amid efforts to cool global warming, the battle hinges on targeting such super-pollutants as methane, which emits one third of the world’s greenhouse gases and “is a fight we can win”, the UN chief on Wednesday.

articles.title: World News in Brief: UN launches Hormuz evacuation plan, UNICEF youth champion killed in Gaza, Lebanon ceasefire ‘largely holding’
articles.summary: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) will begin implementing an evacuation plan for more than 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, the UN agency announced on Tuesday.

articles.title: NASA to Share Latest Moon Base Mission Progress
articles.summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will host a virtual conversation at 2:30 p.m. EDT, Tuesday, June 30, to share updates to NASA’s plans to build a Moon Base on the lunar surface. Administrator Isaacman and Carlos García-Galán, Moon Base program manager, will discuss the next set of awards for new lunar lander missions and preview upcoming […]

articles.title: Roman Telescope Comes to Kennedy
articles.summary: In this June 21, 2026, photo, NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope arrives at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida aboard NASA’s Pegasus barge. After offloading and transportation to the spaceport’s Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility, Roman will undergo processing ahead of launch, targeted no earlier than Sunday, Aug. 30, 2026. Named for NASA’s first […]

articles.title: What Europe’s heat wave means for the power grid
articles.summary: It’s been hard to look away from headlines about the European heat wave this week. Temperatures are breaking records across the continent, and the weather is threatening lives, shutting down schools, and in one particularly ironic case, forcing the cancellation of a London Climate Action Week event about extreme heat. As the summer ramps up…

articles.title: The emergence of the web data infrastructure layer for AI
articles.summary: AI is booming. New use cases are emerging each day. To capitalize on the technology’s potential, enterprises require data at scale. In many cases, though, the relevant information is blocked or unstructured, which limits its use by AI models. To understand this challenge, consider the foundation of the web itself. The web was not designed…

articles.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Smart TV Proxyware, 24-Year curl Bug, AI Crime Forums + 13 More Stories
articles.summary: It’s dumb out there again. This week has the usual smell of prod on fire and nobody wanting to admit who left the door open — old creds still working, trusted apps doing sketchy crap, browser tricks jumping the fence, and “normal” workflows turning into phishing pipes because apparently email was not enough hell already. The worst part is how cheap some of it feels. Not elite. Not cinematic.

articles.title: New Gaslight macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Disrupt AI-Assisted Analysis
articles.summary: A previously undocumented Rust-based macOS implant and information stealer has been found to embed a prompt injection payload designed to trick a malware analyst's artificial intelligence (AI) tools and trick it into aborting or refusing an analysis of the artifact. The malware has been codenamed Gaslight owing to this deceptive behavior. It's been assessed with high confidence that the tool is

articles.title: DoJ Seizes Huione Cloud Account Tied to Cyber Scam Money Laundering
articles.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Tuesday announced the seizure of a cloud computing account put to use by subsidiaries of Cambodia-based corporate conglomerate HuiOne Group, as the Treasury unveiled fresh sanctions against nine individuals and 26 entities linked to Prince Group.
